summary:
• Middle East geopolitical tensions are directly constraining Western Hemisphere supply through Iranian production losses and U.S. crude/gasoline inventory drawdowns that widen the 2026 global oil deficit forecast.

• Russia's sustained high oil revenues despite production sanctions create alternative supply dynamics that could redirect global trade flows and reduce Western Hemisphere market share.

• Fuel shortage pressures are cascading across transportation sectors—from aviation to maritime logistics—threatening to disrupt supply chains and increase energy costs across the Americas region.
